text_original: Motse said: The ancient illustrious kings and sages ruled over the empire and headed the feudal lords because they loved the people loyally and blessed them with many benefits.
text_normalized: Motse said: The ancient illustrious kings and sages ruled over the empire and headed the feudal lords because they loved the people loyally and blessed them with many benefits.

text_original: Loyalty calls out confidence.
text_normalized: Loyalty calls out confidence.

text_original: And, when blessing is shown in addition, the people were not wearied during their whole life and did not feel tired until their death.
text_normalized: And, when blessing is shown in addition, the people were not wearied during their whole life and did not feel tired until their death.

text_original: That the ancient illustrious kings and sages could rule over the empire and head the feudal lords is just because of this.
text_normalized: That the ancient illustrious kings and sages could rule over the empire and head the feudal lords is just because of this.

text_original: And the ancient sage-kings authorized the code of laws of economy, saying: All you artisans and workers, carpenters and tanners, potters and smiths, do what you can do.
text_normalized: And the ancient sage-kings authorized the code of laws of economy, saying: All you artisans and workers, carpenters and tanners, potters and smiths, do what you can do.

text_original: Stop when the needs of the people are satisfied.’' What causes extra expense but adds no benefit to the people the sage-kings would not undertake.
text_normalized: Stop when the needs of the people are satisfied.’' What causes extra expense but adds no benefit to the people the sage-kings would not undertake.

text_original: The ancient sage-kings authorized the code of laws regarding food and drink, saying: Stop when hunger is satiated, breathing becomes strong, limbs are strengthened and ears and eyes become sharp.
text_normalized: The ancient sage-kings authorized the code of laws regarding food and drink, saying: Stop when hunger is satiated, breathing becomes strong, limbs are strengthened and ears and eyes become sharp.

text_original: There is no need of combining the five tastes extremely well or harmonizing the different sweet odours.
text_normalized: There is no need of combining the five tastes extremely well or harmonizing the different sweet odours.

text_original: And efforts should not be made to procure rare delicacies from far countries." How do we know such were the laws?
text_normalized: And efforts should not be made to procure rare delicacies from far countries." How do we know such were the laws?

text_original: In ancient times, when Yao was governing the empire he consolidated Chiao Tse on the south, reached Yu Tu on the north, expanded from where the sun rises to where the sim sets on the east and west, and none was unsubmissive or disrespectful.
text_normalized: In ancient times, when Yao was governing the empire he consolidated Chiao Tse on the south, reached Yu Tu on the north, expanded from where the sun rises to where the sim sets on the east and west, and none was unsubmissive or disrespectful.

text_original: Yet, even when he was served with what he much liked, he did not take a double cereal or both soup and meat.
text_normalized: Yet, even when he was served with what he much liked, he did not take a double cereal or both soup and meat.

text_original: He ate out of an earthen liu Jg and drank out of an earthen hsing and took wine out of a spoon.
text_normalized: He ate out of an earthen liu Jg and drank out of an earthen hsing and took wine out of a spoon.

text_original: With the ceremonies of bowing and stretching and courtesies and decorum the sage-king had nothing to do.
text_normalized: With the ceremonies of bowing and stretching and courtesies and decorum the sage-king had nothing to do.

text_original: The ancient sage-kings authorized the code of laws regarding clothing, saying: " Be satisfied with clothes of blue or grey silk in winter which are light and warm, and with clothes of flax-linen in summer which are light and cool." What causes extra expenditure but does not add benefits to the people the sage-kings would not allow.
text_normalized: The ancient sage-kings authorized the code of laws regarding clothing, saying: " Be satisfied with clothes of blue or grey silk in winter which are light and warm, and with clothes of flax-linen in summer which are light and cool." What causes extra expenditure but does not add benefits to the people the sage-kings would not allow.

text_original: Because the ferocious and cunning animals were destroying men and injuring the people, the ancient sage-kings taught the people the use of weapons, saying: Carry a two-edged sword which penetrates when it pierces and severs when it cuts.
text_normalized: Because the ferocious and cunning animals were destroying men and injuring the people, the ancient sage-kings taught the people the use of weapons, saying: Carry a two-edged sword which penetrates when it pierces and severs when it cuts.

text_original: When struck with the flat side it does not break, this is the utility of a sword.
text_normalized: When struck with the flat side it does not break, this is the utility of a sword.

text_original: To be light and strong and afford convenience of action, such is the utility of an armour." A vehicle is used to carry weight and to travel great distances.
text_normalized: To be light and strong and afford convenience of action, such is the utility of an armour." A vehicle is used to carry weight and to travel great distances.

text_original: It should be safe to ride and easy to pull; safe to ride so as not to hurt the rider, easy to pull so as to reach the destination speedily.
text_normalized: It should be safe to ride and easy to pull; safe to ride so as not to hurt the rider, easy to pull so as to reach the destination speedily.

text_original: This is the utility of the vehicle.
text_normalized: This is the utility of the vehicle.

text_original: Because the wide rivers and broad valleys were once not cross-able the sage-kings ordered boats and oars to be made.
text_normalized: Because the wide rivers and broad valleys were once not cross-able the sage-kings ordered boats and oars to be made.

text_original: And these were made just so that they could cross the rivers.
text_normalized: And these were made just so that they could cross the rivers.

text_original: Even when the High Duke or a feudal lord arrived, the boat and the oars were not changed and the ferryman put on no decorations.
text_normalized: Even when the High Duke or a feudal lord arrived, the boat and the oars were not changed and the ferryman put on no decorations.

text_original: Such is the utility of the boat.
text_normalized: Such is the utility of the boat.

text_original: The ancient sage-kings anthorked the code of laws limiting funeral expenditure, saying: " Of shrouds there shall be three pieces in order to be enough to hold the rotting flesh.
text_normalized: The ancient sage-kings anthorked the code of laws limiting funeral expenditure, saying: " Of shrouds there shall be three pieces in order to be enough to hold the rotting flesh.

text_original: The coffin shall be three inches thick, to be sufficient to hold the rotting bones.
text_normalized: The coffin shall be three inches thick, to be sufficient to hold the rotting bones.

text_original: The pit shall be dug not deep enough to reach water, but just so deep that the gases will not escape.
text_normalized: The pit shall be dug not deep enough to reach water, but just so deep that the gases will not escape.

text_original: When the dead is buried the living shall not mourn too long." In those ancient days, at the beginning of the race, when there were no palaces or houses, people lived in caves dug at the side of hills and mounds.
text_normalized: When the dead is buried the living shall not mourn too long." In those ancient days, at the beginning of the race, when there were no palaces or houses, people lived in caves dug at the side of hills and mounds.

text_original: The sage-kings felt quite concerned, thinking that the caves might keep off the wind and cold in winter, but that in summer it would be wet below and steaming above which might hurt the health of the people.
text_normalized: The sage-kings felt quite concerned, thinking that the caves might keep off the wind and cold in winter, but that in summer it would be wet below and steaming above which might hurt the health of the people.

text_original: So palaces and houses were built and found useful.
text_normalized: So palaces and houses were built and found useful.

text_original: Now, what is the standard in building palaces and houses?
text_normalized: Now, what is the standard in building palaces and houses?

text_original: Motse said: Just so that on the side it can keep off the wind and the cold, on top it can keep off the snow, frost, rain, and dew, within it is clean enough for sacrificial purposes, and that the partition in the palace is high enough to separate the men from the women.
text_normalized: Motse said: Just so that on the side it can keep off the wind and the cold, on top it can keep off the snow, frost, rain, and dew, within it is clean enough for sacrificial purposes, and that the partition in the palace is high enough to separate the men from the women.

text_original: What causes extra expenditure but does not add any benefit, to the people, the sage-kings will not undertake.
text_normalized: What causes extra expenditure but does not add any benefit, to the people, the sage-kings will not undertake.
